md_showcase.description: Markdown rendering showcase — exercises every pattern the inline parser supports. Drop this JSON into ICLI_WEB_TEMPLATES_DIR, then run show_template('md_showcase') to see headers, bold/italic, inline + fenced code, links, lists, blockquotes and paragraphs side by side.

content.title: Markdown showcase
content.md:
# Heading one
## Heading two
### Heading three

Regular paragraph with **bold**, *italic*, and `inline code` inline.

Another paragraph so you can see the gap.

- First list item
- Second with `code`
- Third with a [link to example.com](https://example.com)

> Blockquote: the renderer escapes all HTML before applying patterns, so `<script>` tags land as literal text.

```python
# Fenced code block
def greet(name: str) -> str:
    return f"hi, {name}"

print(greet("icli_web"))
```

Trailing paragraph to confirm the parser closes code fences cleanly.

md_xss_probe.description: XSS probe — renders markdown containing script tags, javascript: URLs, onerror handlers and other injection attempts. If the output shows these as literal text (no alert, no broken DOM), the escape-first strategy is working. Use for manual smoke-testing the orb renderer.

content.title: XSS probe (should render as literal text)
content.md: # Injection attempts `` must render as text. [click me](javascript:alert('xss')) ```html ``` All of the above must appear as visible characters, not executed.
